channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Texture-dependent all-optical switching in ferromagnetic films via stochastic nucleation of nanoscale domains
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 03 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02515-8

Faraday microscopy and magnetic force microscopy with circularly polarized picosecond optical pulses are used to investigate domain switching in a ferromagnetic Pt/Co/Pt thin film. A stochastic model explains the nucleation and growth of magnetic domains, taking into account light helicity and the relative magnetization of neighbouring domains.
item.dc:title: Texture-dependent all-optical switching in ferromagnetic films via stochastic nucleation of nanoscale domains
item.dc:creator: Dinar Khusyainov
Rein Liefferink
MengXing Na
Fabian Kammerbauer
Robert Frömter
Mathias Kläui
Dmitry Kozodaev
Nikolay Vovk
Rostislav V. Mikhaylovskiy
Dmytro Afanasiev
Alexey V. Kimel
Johan H. Mentink
Theo Rasing

item.title: Efficient two-photon emission from a quantum dot
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02523-8

Reliable control over photon number is essential for scalable quantum photonics, yet producing well-defined two-photon emission from quantum emitters remains challenging. Now, by exploiting a dark-state pathway to biexciton population in a quantum-dot microcavity, bright high-purity photon pairs have been generated, offering a promising route towards practical two-photon sources.
item.dc:title: Efficient two-photon emission from a quantum dot

item.title: Stability in the dilute limit
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02527-4

Highly dilute metal dopants arrest nanoparticle sintering by electronically stabilizing a few critical surface atoms, redefining how catalyst stability can be engineered at the atomic scale.
item.dc:title: Stability in the dilute limit
item.dc:creator: Haisong Feng
Xin Zhang
Min Wei

item.title: Metal hybridization in dilute-alloy catalysts promotes sintering resistance by decreasing surface mobility
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02489-7

Single-atom alloys can exhibit unique catalytic properties. Here, using reactivity measurements, atomic-scale imaging, simulation and spectroscopic analysis, it is shown that 1 atom% addition of Pt to Cu nanoparticles substantially decreases deactivation in single-atom alloys via sintering. A general predictive model of catalyst stabilization is developed.
item.dc:title: Metal hybridization in dilute-alloy catalysts promotes sintering resistance by decreasing surface mobility
item.dc:creator: Jordan Finzel
Audrey Dannar
Shoutian Sun
Adam S. Hoffman
Yogita Soni
Bin Wang
Simon R. Bare
E. Charles H. Sykes
Phillip Christopher

item.title: Charge-triggered switching mechanism in selenium selector enabling ultralow leakage current
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02499-5

Phase-change memories require effective ovonic threshold switching (OTS) selectors to eliminate leakage currents. A charge-triggered switching mechanism is identified in amorphous elemental selenium and shown to make it an effective OTS selector that can achieve a leakage current of 4 × 10−12 A.
item.dc:title: Charge-triggered switching mechanism in selenium selector enabling ultralow leakage current
item.dc:creator: Yuting Sun
Tamihiro Gotoh
Jiayi Zhao
Mengfei Zhang
Shucheng Shi
Hui Zhang
Zhi Liu
Jiabin Shen
Richard Dronskowski
Zhitang Song
Stephen R. Elliott
Min Zhu

item.title: Purcell-enhanced two-photon emission from a quantum dot via dark-state biexciton loading
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02522-9

Resonant p-shell excitation of a Purcell-enhanced In(Ga)As quantum dot–micropillar system enables efficient biexciton loading, leading to efficient two-photon emission with strong two-photon correlations and a high two-photon fraction.
item.dc:title: Purcell-enhanced two-photon emission from a quantum dot via dark-state biexciton loading
item.dc:creator: Bang Wu
Li Liu
Hanqing Liu
Xinrui Mao
Xu-Jie Wang
Haiqiao Ni
Zhichuan Niu
Zhiliang Yuan

item.title: Deeper insights into charge photogeneration in organic solar cells
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02500-1

An advanced model is developed to pinpoint the parameters that control the bulk charge photogeneration yield and to provide design strategies for highly efficient organic solar cells.
item.dc:title: Deeper insights into charge photogeneration in organic solar cells
item.dc:creator: Carsten Deibel
